entries.title: Commentary on Proverbs on proverbs 16:2
entries.excerpt: All the ways of a man are open to His eyes, etc. Thus the ways, that is, the actions of all, both good and evil, the Lord sees, so that He distinguishes with a certain scale even the individual spirits, what they secretly think. Hence it is rightly added:

entries.title: COMMENTARY ON THE PROVERBS OF SOLOMON, FRAGMENT 16:2 on proverbs 16:2
entries.excerpt: Besides being bright, certainly light (as it is) is conspicuous. As it does its work everyone observes it. In the same way, the humble person shows us in no small way what is right. For a contrite person will nevertheless excel in great things. But God does not desire to acknowledge the deeds of the proud.
